turns.content: bro i gotta tell you something
What's up?
i passed
Passed what? Don't leave me hanging!
the real estate exam bro. i passed it. wallahi i almost cried in the testing center
Congratulations!! That's huge. All those hours studying while driving all day — you actually did it. How are you feeling?
bro im not gonna lie im shaking a little still. like this has been the plan for so long and now its real. its actually happening
It IS real. You put in the work and earned it. This is a genuine turning point. So what's the next step — finding a brokerage to work with?
already done bro. i talked to a broker at century 21 a few weeks ago and they said once i pass they got a spot for me. i start april 1st
Wait, you already had the brokerage lined up? That's smart — no gap between passing and getting started. Century 21 is a solid name too. Where's the office?
energy corridor. which is good cuz thats a busy area. lots of people moving in and out with the oil companies
Energy corridor is a great spot for real estate. Constant turnover with relocations, good price range properties, and you already know that area from driving. That's a real advantage going in.
thats exactly what i was thinking. i know those neighborhoods bro. i been driving through them for years. i know which streets are nice which complexes are trash

That ground-level knowledge is going to set you apart from agents who just look at listings on a screen. You've literally driven every block out there. Are you planning to keep driving Uber while you build up the real estate side?
yeah obviously gotta keep uber going. real estate takes time before you see real money. but at least now i got a path bro. like an actual career not just grinding rides

That's the key difference. Uber keeps the bills paid while you build something that can actually grow. A lot of new agents burn out because they don't have that safety net. You're set up the right way.
inshallah bro. im excited and scared at the same time. like what if i cant close deals. what if nobody wants to work with me

That fear is normal and honestly a good sign — it means you're taking it seriously. But think about it: you know the city better than most agents, you know how to hustle, and you connect with people every single day in the car. You've been building those skills without even realizing it.
yeah thats true. the hustle part im not worried about. its the business side. contracts paperwork all that

That's what the brokerage is for — Century 21 will have training, mentors, and support for new agents. You don't have to figure out the paperwork alone. Lean on them hard in the first few months. That's literally what they're there for.
you right. ok im done stressing. this is a good day bro. wallahi this is the best day ive had in a long time
It IS a good day. You deserve to just sit with that for a minute. April 1st is going to be the start of something big.
